handoff_rows.name: GE Vernova on grid equipment and transformer buildout
handoff_rows.canonical_entity_name: GE Vernova on grid equipment and transformer buildout
handoff_rows.thesis: GE Vernova appears to sit in a constrained grid equipment and transformer buildout layer, with filing-backed component specificity, pressure signals, and capacity-expansion evidence that may still be underappreciated.
handoff_rows.value_capture_layer: Constrained upstream component supplier with system-level leverage
top_catalysts: order backlog conversion into reported revenue
top_invalidations: capacity expansion arrives without meaningful demand conversion
component or backlog signals fade in subsequent company reporting
downstream demand shifts to substitute suppliers or adjacent architectures
why_missed: market attention is concentrated on downstream power and AI beneficiaries
constrained component-layer suppliers are less obvious from headline buildout coverage
handoff_rows.execution_readiness_note: Upstream constrained-layer exposure with direct U.S. execution path.

handoff_rows.name: Eaton on grid equipment and transformer buildout
handoff_rows.canonical_entity_name: Eaton on grid equipment and transformer buildout
handoff_rows.thesis: Eaton appears to be a relevant beneficiary of grid equipment and transformer buildout, but the current evidence supports a beneficiary posture more cleanly than a strict bottleneck claim.
handoff_rows.value_capture_layer: Relevant supply-chain beneficiary without strict bottleneck proof
top_catalysts: order backlog conversion into reported revenue
top_invalidations: capacity expansion arrives without meaningful demand conversion
component or backlog signals fade in subsequent company reporting
downstream demand shifts to substitute suppliers or adjacent architectures
why_missed: beneficiary role is real but not yet framed as a strict chokepoint thesis
supplier relevance is easier to miss when coverage emphasizes end-market demand instead of component exposure
handoff_rows.execution_readiness_note: Supplier exposure is actionable, but bottleneck proof is weaker than the top queue name.
